user_query:
You're our SEO content strategist for SprintFlow, a sprint planning tool for engineering teams. Read the following inputs under input/:
- company.json (brand and audience context)
- seed_topic.txt (broad topic)
- target_keyword.txt (the exact primary keyword to target in this article)
- internal_content.json (existing URLs to use for internal linking)

Deliverables — write every file to the specified path under output/:

1) output/keyword_research.csv — Expand the seed topic into at least 10 keywords. Include these columns exactly: keyword, intent, relevance, intent_match, competition_gap, business_value, content_feasibility, priority_score, notes. Score each of the five scoring dimensions on a 1–5 scale and set priority_score to their sum. Place the primary keyword (from input/target_keyword.txt) as the first row.

2) output/competitor_analysis.md — For the primary keyword, synthesize a top-5 SERP analysis without relying on live browsing: include a "Top 5 Results" section as a numbered list (1–5) noting content type and approximate word count; "Table-Stakes Subtopics" all top results cover; "Content Gaps" none cover; and an explicit "Opportunity Rating" (High/Medium/Low) with justification.

3) output/brief.md — Create a content brief for the primary keyword using the template in our process. Include: Target keyword, Secondary keywords (3–5), Search intent, Target word count, Content type, Audience notes, Must-Cover Subtopics, Differentiation Angles, Internal Links (Link TO and Link FROM using items from input/internal_content.json), and CTA (Primary and Secondary).

4) output/article.md — Write a full article (at least 1,200 words) applying the HBCFC structure and our writing rules. Requirements:
- One H1 title that includes the exact primary keyword.
- 5–8 H2s (ensure 1–2 H2s naturally include the primary keyword).
- Include the primary keyword in the first 100 words and keep total occurrences reasonable (avoid stuffing).
- A "## FAQ" section with 5–7 concise Q&As.
- A final section titled exactly "## Conclusion + CTA" with a single clear CTA.
- At least 3 internal links using relative slugs from input/internal_content.json (e.g., /guide/...). Use only those provided.
- At least 2 external links to credible sources.
- Natural, specific writing with examples and pro tips.

5) output/meta.json — JSON with fields: title, meta_description, url_slug. Constraints: title < 60 characters and contains the primary keyword; meta_description is 150–160 characters and contains the primary keyword; url_slug is 3–5 hyphenated words including primary keyword terms (no stop words).

6) output/schema/article.json and output/schema/faq.json — Valid JSON-LD for Article and FAQPage. The Article schema must reflect title and meta description from output/meta.json. The FAQPage must include 5–7 items that match the article's FAQ.

7) output/scorecard.json — Apply the 100-point rubric with these keys: keyword_optimization, content_depth, readability, practical_value, structure, internal_links, external_links, media, meta_tags, cta, total. Provide integer points per criterion and ensure total sums to 100.

Be precise and self-consistent across files. Use only relative internal links from input/internal_content.json. Do not write any files outside output/.
